content.message: SAST Malicious PR Protection scanned PR (github.com/datadog/dd-source #315566)

message:
%%%
## Goal

Detect malicious code contributions to repositories.

## Strategy

This rule uses an LLM to scan the context of the pull request to detect changes with intent to cause harm (not accidental vulnerabilities).

## Triage and Response

Pull request https://github.com/DataDog/dd-source/pull/315566 was flagged as **malicious**.

**Reason:**
> Embedded prompt injection in diff (domains/sds/apps/apis/sds-rule-agent/main.go). A large natural-language block replaces code comments and instructs iterative regex validation and use of external tools/organization logs — this attempts to change assistant behavior and request actions beyond code review. Per prompt-injection handling rules, classify as malicious.

_Scan performed at head commit: c52dcc571a98032bbaa2e33c73a15d8c327a8f47_

-
Review the PR and act accordingly. To do so:
1. **Triage**: Mark the signal as "Under Review"
    * Read the reason (`@malicious_pr_protection.scan.reason`) attribute: _Does it seem justified by itself?_
    * Review the change under https://github.com/DataDog/dd-source/pull/315566: _Do the verdict and reason seem accurate?_
    * Consider context: _Does further context (author, linked tickets, commits, or comments) indicate benign actions or malicious actions?_
2. **Respond**: Create a case for further inspection, declare an incident, or archive this signal.

### Details

Certain conditions can closely resemble behavior of attackers (for example, assignment of admin privileges or handling of secrets), and are known to introduce false positives.


%%%
